errors: ; Age Threshold: CPG requires age >= 18 years, ELM implementation requires age >= 25 years.; Lookback Period: CPG requires a lookback period of 12 months (1 year), ELM implementation uses a lookback period of 6 months.; Value Set Reference: CPG uses the specific code "2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1920" for Encounter to Screen for Blood Pressure, ELM implementation uses a generic reference to the same value set without the specific code.

; Age range in ELM implementation is 30-80 years, while the CPG requires 30-85 years.; The LDL Cholesterol value set reference in ELM implementation matches the CPG (2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.3.1573).; The logic for determining the most recent LDL Cholesterol value is not specified in the ELM implementation.; The ELM implementation does not include a time interval for the LDL Cholesterol value check (190 mg/dL).; The ELM implementation does not specify the logic for determining if the patient's age is within the specified range.; The ELM implementation does not include a link to the CPG or suggestions for further action.; The ELM implementation does not specify any errors to be returned when criteria are not met.

; Age Threshold: ELM implementation specifies age >= 75 years, while the CPG requires age >= 65 years.; Time Intervals: ELM implementation specifies a 1-year lookback period for recent falls assessment, which matches the CPG.; Value Sets: Both ELM and CPG reference the same value set for fall risk assessment.; Logic Definitions: The ELM implementation's logic for determining the need for falls prevention screening does not match the CPG's logic. The CPG requires that the patient is 65 years or older and has not had a falls risk assessment in the past year, whereas the ELM implementation requires the patient to be 75 years or older and have not had a falls risk assessment in the past year.; Decision Logic: The ELM implementation's decision logic does not correctly implement the CPG's decision logic. The CPG requires that the patient meets both inclusion criteria (age >= 65 years and no falls risk assessment in the past year), while the ELM implementation only requires one of the criteria (age >= 75 years).
